frameworks.name: signal-to-noise modulation
frameworks.one_line: NIBS indiscriminately activates neurons, shifting signal-versus-noise balance in targeted circuits.

frameworks.name: lesion-method analogy
frameworks.one_line: NIBS treated as transient virtual lesion to infer causal role of regions.

frameworks.name: state-dependent stimulation
frameworks.one_line: Behavioural effect of NIBS depends on prevailing neural activity state.

core_question: How should non-invasive brain stimulation be theoretically framed so that its effects on neural activity inform causal claims about cognitive processes?
key_assumptions: NIBS transiently alters neuronal activity in stimulated regions
Large numbers of neurons are activated relatively indiscriminately
Behavioural changes reflect modulation of underlying neural computation
manipulations: Application of non-invasive brain stimulation to targeted cortical regions
Comparison of facilitatory versus inhibitory stimulation protocols
key_findings: NIBS can establish causal links between brain regions and cognitive processes, beyond correlation
Effects can be cast as modulation of the signal-to-noise relation in stimulated circuits
NIBS yields information about where, when, and how a region contributes to cognition
interpretations: NIBS extends lesion-style inference by adding temporal and mechanistic resolution
Understanding NIBS requires joint theory of the stimulation method and the targeted neural system
limitations_noted: Stimulation activates large neuronal populations relatively indiscriminately
